message:
fix(engine): extract effective_cost_millionths to prevent zero/negative-cost budget exploits

The monitor scheduler used inline .max(1) clamping in voi_score() to
prevent division by zero, but the schedule_round budget accounting path
read cost_millionths directly. A probe configured with zero or negative
cost could pass scheduling without consuming any budget, effectively
allowing unlimited probe execution.

Changes:
- Extract ProbeConfig::effective_cost_millionths() method that clamps
  non-positive values to 1, documented as preventing budget minting
- Replace all .max(1) and direct cost_millionths reads in voi_score()
  and schedule_round() with effective_cost_millionths()
- Add effective_cost_clamps_non_positive_values test covering zero,
  negative, and positive inputs
- Add schedule_negative_cost_probe_uses_floor_for_budget_accounting
  test verifying budget is correctly consumed even for negative-cost
  probes

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
